deep_dive.title: Interference-Enhanced Large Electron-Phonon Coupling from Raman-active Breathing Modes in Moiré Semiconductors
deep_dive.abstract: Superconductivity was recently observed in twisted WSe2 and MoTe2, raising a central question: is the pairing driven by electronic correlations, by phonons, or by both? Answering it requires determining the electron-phonon coupling (EPC) in these moiré semiconductors, whose calculation in realistic supercells of thousands of atoms lies beyond the reach of direct first-principles methods. Here we combine filling-dependent Raman spectroscopy with machine-learning first-principles calculations to obtain the EPC mode by mode in supercells of up to tens of thousands of atoms. Raman reveals only a few moiré phonons whose frequencies shift strongly with filling; we trace this to an interference selection rule: a phonon couples strongly only when its displacement texture matches the static lattice-reconstruction pattern, and is otherwise suppressed by destructive interference. The rule selects the low- and high-frequency breathing modes seen in Raman and makes the coupling peak at large twist angles, near those at which superconductivity appears. Lattice-reconstruction interference thus emerges as an organizing principle for moiré EPC, pointing to a substantial, potentially dominant, phonon contribution to large-angle pairing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: machine-learning first-principles calculations for electron-phonon coupling in moire semiconductors
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 机器学习第一性原理计算用于摩尔半导体中的电子-声子耦合
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 机器学习第一性原理计算用于摩尔半导体中的电子-声子耦合

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This work combines Raman spectroscopy with machine-learning first-principles calculations to determine electron-phonon coupling mode by mode in moire semiconductors, enabling first-principles-level accuracy in supercells of tens of thousands of atoms.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 摩尔半导体中的超导机制需要准确电子-声子耦合，但传统第一性原理无法处理数千原子超胞。本文结合填充依赖拉曼光谱与机器学习第一性原理计算，逐模式提取电子-声子耦合。在数万原子超胞中实现了第一性原理精度，揭示了干涉选择规则主导的强耦合模式。该方法为研究大尺度体系电子-声子相互作用提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 摩尔半导体中的超导机制需要准确电子-声子耦合，但传统第一性原理无法处理数千原子超胞。本文结合填充依赖拉曼光谱与机器学习第一性原理计算，逐模式提取电子-声子耦合。在数万原子超胞中实现了第一性原理精度，揭示了干涉选择规则主导的强耦合模式。该方法为研究大尺度体系电子-声子相互作用提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Interpretable AI driven materiomics to decode microenvironmental cues for stem cell immunomodulation
quick_skim.abstract: Hydrogels that mimic the extracellular matrix create microenvironments containing diverse physicochemical cues that regulate stem cell fate, particularly their immunomodulatory and pro-regenerative functions. However, elucidating how microenvironmental cues regulate cell fate remains challenging because of complex multi-parameter interactions and nonlinear relationships, thereby requiring large numbers of samples and substantial experimental effort. Here, we present a materiomics platform that integrates a large-scale systematic dataset with artificial intelligence (AI) to evaluate and predict optimal stem cell niche features for enhancing the immunomodulatory functions of mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs). Specifically, binary hydrogels composed of methacrylated alginate and gelatin (AMGM) were used to construct a comprehensive materiomics database incorporating seven critical physicochemical cues associated with MSC-mediated regulation of macrophage polarization. Machine learning models trained on this materiomics database successfully predicted hydrogel formulations that enhanced MSC immunomodulatory efficacy and further identified matrix stiffness as the physicochemical cue with the greatest contribution to MSC-mediated immunomodulation within the AMGM hydrogel system. In summary, this study demonstrates the utility of AI for evaluating the relative contributions of microenvironmental cues and establishes a data-driven research framework for investigating microenvironmental regulation of stem cell function.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: artificial intelligence in materials science research: AI-driven materiomics for stem cell microenvironments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 材料科学研究中的人工智能：AI驱动的材料组学研究干细胞微环境
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 材料科学研究中的人工智能：AI驱动的材料组学研究干细胞微环境
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 材料科学研究中的人工智能：AI驱动的材料组学研究干细胞微环境
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a materiomics platform integrating large-scale dataset with AI to predict optimal stem cell niche features for immunomodulation, using interpretable machine learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对干细胞微环境中复杂多参数相互作用难以解析的问题，提出了一个材料组学平台，整合大规模系统数据集与可解释人工智能，利用机器学习模型评估和预测最佳干细胞生态位特征。该方法成功预测了增强间充质干细胞免疫调节功能的关键微环境信号，揭示了理化因素的非线性调控关系，大幅减少实验次数，为再生医学中的智能生物材料设计提供了数据驱动的新范式，展现了AI在生物材料科学中的应用潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该文针对干细胞微环境中复杂多参数相互作用难以解析的问题，提出了一个材料组学平台，整合大规模系统数据集与可解释人工智能，利用机器学习模型评估和预测最佳干细胞生态位特征。该方法成功预测了增强间充质干细胞免疫调节功能的关键微环境信号，揭示了理化因素的非线性调控关系，大幅减少实验次数，为再生医学中的智能生物材料设计提供了数据驱动的新范式，展现了AI在生物材料科学中的应用潜力。
